participant_id.Description: The subject ID of the participant

age.Description: The age of the participant in years.

sex.Description: The self-reported gender of the participant.

ethnicity.Description: The self-reported ethnicity of the participant.

Hand.Description: The dominant hand of the participant (left or right).

SW_sum.Description: Sum score for society works best scale

pol_orient.Description: Political orientation of the participant (1 extremely liberal - 100 extremely conservative).

MFTA1_1.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone suffered emotionally.

MFTA2_8.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone cared for someone weak or vulnerable.

MFTA3_8.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone was cruel.

MFTB1_1.Description: Response to MFQ-Compassion for those who are suffering is the most crucial virtue.

MFTB2_2.Description: Response to MFQ-One of the worst things a person could do is hurt a defenseless animal.

MFTB4_2.Description: Response to MFQ-It can never be right to kill a human being.

MFTA1_2.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not some people were treated differently than others.

MFTA2_6.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone acted unfairly.

MFTB1_2.Description: Response to MFQ-When the government makes laws, the number one principle should be ensuring that everyone is treated fairly.

MFTB2_3.Description: Response to MFQ-Justice is the most important requirement for a society.

MFTB4_3.Description: Response to MFQ-I think it’s morally wrong that rich children inherit a lot of money while poor children inherit nothing.

MFTA1_3.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone’s action showed love for his or her country.

MFTA2_3.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone did something to betray his or her group.

MFTA3_2.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone showed a lack of loyalty.

MFTB1_3.Description: Response to MFQ-Justice is the most important requirement for a society.

MFTB2_4.Description: Response to MFQ-People should be loyal to their family members, even when they have done something wrong.

MFTB4_4.Description: Response to MFQ-It is more important to be a team player than to express oneself.

MFTA1_4.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone showed a lack of respect for authority.

MFTA2_2.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone conformed to the traditions of society.

MFTA3_12.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not an action caused chaos or disorder.

MFTB1_4.Description: Response to MFQ-Respect for authority is something all children need to learn.

MFTB2_5.Description: Response to MFQ-Men and women each have different roles to play in society.

MFTB4_5.Description: Response to MFQ-If I were a soldier and disagreed with my commanding officers orders, I would obey anyway because that is my duty.

MFTA1_5.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone violated standards of purity and decency.

MFTA3_7.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone did something disgusting.

MFTA3_13.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone acted in a way that God would approve of.

MFTB1_5.Description: Response to MFQ-People should not do things that are disgusting, even if no one is harmed.

MFTB4_1.Description: Response to MFQ-I would call some acts wrong on the grounds that they are unnatural.

MFTB4_6.Description: Response to MFQ-Chastity is an important and valuable virtue.

MFTA3_3.Description: Response to MFQ-Whether or not someone was denied their rights.

MFTA2_7.Description: Response to MFQ CATCH-Whether or not someone was good at math

MFTB2_1.Description: Response to MFQ CATCH-It is better to do good than bad.

mfq_care_avg.Description: Average score for the care/harm foundation in the Moral Foundations Questionnaire (MFQ).

mfq_fairness_avg.Description: Average score for the fairness/cheating foundation in the MFQ.

mfq_loyalty_avg.Description: Average score for the loyalty/betrayal foundation in the MFQ.

mfq_authority_avg.Description: Average score for the authority/subversion foundation in the MFQ.

mfq_sanctity_avg.Description: Average score for the sanctity/degradation foundation in the MFQ.

E5_2.Description: Response to IRI-When I am reading an interesting story or novel, I imagine how I would feel if the events in the story were happening to me.

E1_5.Description: Response to IRI-I really get involved with the feelings of the characters in a novel.

E2_1.Description: Response to IRI-I am usually objective when I watch a movie or play, and I don't often get completely caught up in it.

E3_4.Description: Response to IRI-After seeing a play or movie, I have felt as though I were one of the characters.

E1_1.Description: Response to IRI-I daydream and fantasize, with some regularity, about things that might happen to me.

E2_6.Description: Response to IRI-Becoming extremely involved in a good book or movie is somewhat rare for me.

E4_5.Description: Response to IRI-When I watch a good movie, I can very easily put myself in the place of a leading character.

E5_4.Description: Response to IRI-Before criticizing somebody, I try to imagine how I would feel if I were in their place.

E3_3.Description: Response to IRI-If I'm sure I'm right about something, I don't waste much time listening to other people's arguments.

E2_5.Description: Response to IRI-I sometimes try to understand my friends better by imagining how things look from their perspective.

E4_3.Description: Response to IRI-I believe that there are two sides to every question and try to look at them both.

E1_3.Description: Response to IRI-I sometimes find it difficult to see things from the 'other guy's' point of view.

E2_2.Description: Response to IRI-I try to look at everybody's side of a disagreement before I make a decision.

E5_1.Description: Response to IRI-When I'm upset at someone, I usually try to 'put myself in his shoes' for a while.

E2_3.Description: Response to IRI-When I see someone being taken advantage of, I feel kind of protective towards them.

E3_6.Description: Response to IRI-When I see someone being treated unfairly, I sometimes don't feel very much pity for them.

E1_2.Description: Response to IRI-I often have tender, concerned feelings for people less fortunate than me.

E4_4.Description: Response to IRI-I would describe myself as a pretty soft-hearted person.

E1_4.Description: Response to IRI-Sometimes I don't feel very sorry for other people when they are having problems.

E3_2.Description: Response to IRI-Other people's misfortunes do not usually disturb me a great deal.

E4_2.Description: Response to IRI-I am often quite touched by things that I see happen.

E5_3.Description: Response to IRI-When I see someone who badly needs help in an emergency, I go to pieces.

E2_4.Description: Response to IRI-I sometimes feel helpless when I am in the middle of a very emotional situation.

E1_6.Description: Response to IRI-In emergency situations, I feel apprehensive and ill-at-ease.

E4_1.Description: Response to IRI-I am usually pretty effective in dealing with emergencies.

E3_5.Description: Response to IRI-Being in a tense emotional situation scares me.

E3_1.Description: Response to IRI-When I see someone get hurt, I tend to remain calm.

E4_6.Description: Response to IRI-I tend to lose control during emergencies.

iri_fantasy_avg.Description: Average score for the Fantasy subscale of the Interpersonal Reactivity Index (IRI).

iri_perspective_avg.Description: Average score for the Perspective Taking subscale of the IRI.

iri_concern_avg.Description: Average score for the Empathic Concern subscale of the IRI.

iri_distress_avg.Description: Average score for the Empathic Distress subscale of the IRI.
